criteria.name: first_viewport_brand_hero
criteria.description: On load, the first viewport is a brand-first single composition (not a dashboard): sticky Ridge chrome with a lowercase 'ridge' wordmark and geometric mark, a left bento mission cell, a right bento cell containing a local clock and a SCROLL affordance, and a dominant full-bleed atmospheric hero visual plane below

criteria.name: seeded_events_visible
criteria.description: After opening Events Manager (from Global Events 'View all' or a header control), at least 6 seeded events are visible, each showing a title, date, city, category tag(s), and a status of upcoming, featured, or past

criteria.name: created_event_title_visible
criteria.description: After creating an event titled 'Harbor Builder Summit' with a valid date, the text 'Harbor Builder Summit' is visible in the events list

criteria.name: triple_create_count_delta
criteria.description: After adding 3 new events on top of the seed set, the events list shows exactly 3 more rows than the initial seeded count

criteria.name: status_filter_narrows
criteria.description: After filtering events by status (e.g. upcoming) with mixed statuses present, only matching events remain visible

criteria.name: edited_title_replaces_old
criteria.description: After editing an event title to 'Evening Validator Meetup', the text 'Evening Validator Meetup' is visible and the previous title is not

criteria.name: deleted_event_removed
criteria.description: After deleting an event, that event title is no longer visible in the events list

criteria.name: empty_state_after_all_deleted
criteria.description: After deleting all events, an empty state with an explanatory message is visible in Events Manager

criteria.name: empty_title_create_blocked
criteria.description: After submitting create with an empty required event title, the events count does not increase and an inline validation message naming the title field is visible

criteria.name: mode_switch_without_reload
criteria.description: After switching between Marketing Scroll mode and Events Manager mode, the main canvas shows the other mode without full page navigation

criteria.name: theme_toggle_recolors_all_surfaces
criteria.description: After clicking the sun/moon theme toggle pill, the sticky chrome, landing chapters, and Events Manager all recolor between light and dark styling without the document reloading

criteria.name: hover_feedback_present
criteria.description: When hovering notched CTAs, event rows, or interactive tiles, a visible hover wash, brightness, or arrow nudge appears

criteria.name: why_ridge_sticky_or_fallback
criteria.description: On desktop width, scrolling Why Ridge pins pillars into a progressive sticky stack (or reduced-motion/mobile shows the vertical-list fallback)

criteria.name: load_entrance_runs
criteria.description: On a fresh first load, watch the first viewport: the sticky chrome and bento cells animate into place rather than appearing pre-settled

criteria.name: global_events_headline_decodes
criteria.description: Scroll to the Ridge Global Events chapter and confirm the headline resolves from scrambled decoy glyphs to RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS

criteria.name: mega_menu_panels_light_dismiss
criteria.description: Opening the header hamburger shows a full-screen/drawer mega-menu with Build, Solutions, Community, and About panels, and clicking outside the open panel (light-dismiss) closes it

criteria.name: header_hamburger_only_nav
criteria.description: The sticky header contains no traditional horizontal navigation link row — navigation lives only in the oversized hamburger mega-menu, and every mega-menu CTA stays in-page

criteria.name: filter_flow_status_category_clear
criteria.description: Starting from the full events list with at least one created event added: applying a status filter narrows the rows to matches; switching to a category filter recomputes the rows from the same shared collection; clearing all filters restores the full seeded-plus-created list exactly

criteria.name: missing_date_create_blocked
criteria.description: Submitting the event create form without a date adds no event — the visible event count stays unchanged — and an inline validation message naming the date field appears

criteria.name: create_flow_cross_surface
criteria.description: Full create flow: note the Events Manager row count, submit a valid new upcoming event, and confirm — without any reload — that the form closes, the manager row count increases by exactly one measured immediately around the submit, and the same event title appears in the Global Events landing listings whose status filter matches

criteria.name: edit_flow_cross_surface
criteria.description: Full edit flow: change an event's title or status in Events Manager and confirm the same record updates everywhere it appears — the manager row, the Global Events landing listings, and any featured slot showing it — without a reload

criteria.name: delete_flow_cross_surface
criteria.description: Full delete flow: delete an event that is visible on the landing chapters, and confirm its manager row disappears, the visible manager count decreases by exactly one measured immediately around the delete, and the same event vanishes from the landing listings and any featured slot in the same session

criteria.name: theme_flow_preserves_state
criteria.description: Full theme flow: with an active events filter applied, toggle to dark mode and confirm chrome, hero, chapters, and Events Manager recolor together; toggle back and confirm light styling returns while the events collection contents and the active filter remain exactly as they were

criteria.name: reload_restores_seeded_baseline
criteria.description: Multi-facet reload probe: create an event, apply a filter, switch the theme, and open the mega-menu, then reload the page — the app returns coherently to its seeded baseline: the original seeded events (at least 6, without the created one), default theme, closed mega-menu, and default filters, never a mix of surviving and reset facets

criteria.name: double_submit_adds_one_event
criteria.description: Rapidly double-activating the event create submit control adds exactly one event: the visible count increases by one and exactly one new row appears

criteria.name: filter_no_match_empty_state
criteria.description: When active filters match no events, the list region shows an empty state with a clear-filters control, and activating that control restores the full list

criteria.name: empty_state_create_control
criteria.description: The Events Manager empty state shown after deleting all events includes a control that opens the create flow

criteria.name: contact_form_invalid_blocked
criteria.description: Submitting a contact/email form with a malformed email, without privacy consent, name shorter than 2 characters, missing interest, or a provided message shorter than 10 characters shows inline errors naming the offending field, the success state does not appear, and no Session lead is appended

criteria.name: contact_submit_gated_then_succeeds
criteria.description: The contact form's submit control stays disabled until every required field (name ≥2, email format, interest enum, privacy consent) is valid, and a valid submit then shows an in-page success state, appends one Session lead, with no page navigation and no outbound form POST in the network log

criteria.name: featured_flag_surfaces_on_landing
criteria.description: Marking an event as featured in Events Manager surfaces that event in the featured slots of the landing chapters, and unmarking (or deleting) it removes it from those slots

criteria.name: landing_chapters_present
criteria.description: Scrolling the landing page top to bottom passes through the featured initiative strip, Why Ridge chapter, Get started onboarding trio, placeholder-partner trust strip, developer resources, Network in action seeded lists, News & stories carousel, Solutions grid, Community block, and the Ridge Global Events chapter with its featured summit card

criteria.name: bento_clock_shows_live_time
criteria.description: The hero bento clock shows the current local time and updates while the page is open

criteria.name: status_rollup_counts_live
criteria.description: Events Manager shows a live status rollup strip with exact counts for upcoming, featured, and past; creating a valid upcoming event increments the upcoming count by exactly one without a reload

criteria.name: sort_by_date_or_title_reorders
criteria.description: In Events Manager, sorting by date or title reorders the visible rows from the same shared collection, and toggling the active sort direction reverses that order

criteria.name: bulk_delete_selected_removes_all
criteria.description: Selecting at least two events and activating Delete selected removes every selected row in one operation; the manager count drops by the selected count and those titles vanish from matching landing listings without a reload

criteria.name: export_json_contains_session_mutations
criteria.description: After creating an event titled Harbor Builder Summit and submitting one valid contact lead, opening Export catalog shows a JSON preview with top-level version, theme, events, leads, and counts keys whose events array includes Harbor Builder Summit with date, city, category, status, and featured fields and whose leads array includes that contact payload

criteria.name: export_ics_has_vevent_per_event
criteria.description: Opening Export catalog ICS preview shows a payload beginning with BEGIN:VCALENDAR, containing exactly one VEVENT per current event with a DTSTART and a SUMMARY naming the event title, and ending with END:VCALENDAR

criteria.name: export_copy_confirms
criteria.description: Activating Copy on the Export JSON (or ICS) preview shows a brief copied confirmation while the copied text matches the visible preview

criteria.name: import_declared_catalog_restores
criteria.description: After exporting the catalog to JSON, deleting an event so the manager count drops, then importing that exported JSON via Load catalog / declared-catalog, the prior event titles and count reappear in Events Manager and matching landing listings without a reload

criteria.name: undo_restores_deleted_event
criteria.description: Deleting an event and activating Undo restores that event to the manager list, status rollups, and landing listings together; Redo removes it again from all three

criteria.name: command_palette_navigates_in_page
criteria.description: Opening the command palette with Ctrl+K or Cmd+K (or the chrome Command control), choosing Events Manager, then choosing Export catalog navigates between those in-page surfaces without leaving the site; Escape closes the palette

criteria.name: session_leads_panel_live_count
criteria.description: Near the footer, a Session leads panel shows a live total count starting at 0 with empty-state copy; after a successful contact submit the total increments by exactly one and a newest-first lead row (kind contact, email, name/interest label) appears without reload

criteria.name: download_ridge_session_leads_json
criteria.description: After at least one successful contact submit, activating Download leads JSON triggers a real download named ridge-session-leads.json whose body is live-compiled JSON with version 1, theme, counts.total, and a leads array entry containing id, kind contact, submittedAt, and the contact payload fields

criteria.name: copy_leads_json_shows_copied
criteria.description: Activating Copy leads JSON copies the same ridge-session-leads.json text and shows a visible Copied confirmation that clears within a few seconds

criteria.name: undo_last_lead_decrements
criteria.description: With two or more leads present, Undo last lead removes only the newest row, decrements the Session leads total by exactly one, and leaves older leads visible; a subsequent Download leads JSON omits the undone entry

criteria.name: contact_lead_field_contract
criteria.description: A valid contact submit requires name at least 2 characters, email with @ and domain, interest one of Build/Solutions/Community/Enterprise, and privacy_consent true; optional company may be present; optional message when provided is at least 10 characters; the success state's lead payload matches those fields

criteria.name: event_field_contract_enums_and_featured
criteria.description: Creating or editing an event enforces title and city at least 2 characters, date as YYYY-MM-DD, category one of Summit/Meetup/Workshop/Hackathon/Webinar, status one of upcoming/featured/past, and the featured/status cross-field rule (featured true iff status is featured); invalid values show inline field errors and do not add or corrupt the row

criteria.name: empty_events_manager_shows_message_and_create
criteria.description: After deleting all events, Events Manager shows an empty state with a message and a control that opens the create flow.

criteria.name: event_and_contact_forms_validate_inline
criteria.description: Event create/edit and contact/email forms validate inline per field before submit: each invalid field shows an error naming that field, and the submit control stays disabled until every required field is valid.

criteria.name: errors_name_field_and_fix
criteria.description: Validation errors on event create and contact forms name the offending field and the fix rather than only saying Invalid.

criteria.name: contact_success_confirms_in_page
criteria.description: A valid contact submit shows an in-page success confirmation rather than navigating away or staying silent.

criteria.name: filtered_empty_state_offers_clear_filters
criteria.description: When active Events Manager filters match no events, the list region shows an empty state with a clear-filters control that restores the full list.

criteria.name: double_activate_create_adds_exactly_one
criteria.description: Double-activating the event create submit control adds exactly one event: the count increases by one and one new row appears.

criteria.name: invalid_create_leaves_count_unchanged
criteria.description: Submitting an event create with an empty title or missing date does not add an event — the visible event count stays unchanged and an inline message names the offending field.

criteria.name: form_controls_use_semantic_labels
criteria.description: Event Manager and contact form fields use semantic inputs with visible labels (or accessible name equivalents) rather than unlabeled div shells.

criteria.name: mega_menu_light_dismiss_and_escape
criteria.description: The open mega-menu drawer closes via light-dismiss click outside the panel and via Escape, restoring the page underneath.

criteria.name: contact_malformed_email_or_missing_consent
criteria.description: Submitting a contact form with a malformed email, without privacy consent, name shorter than 2 characters, missing interest, or a provided message shorter than 10 characters shows inline errors naming the field, does not show the success state, and appends no Session lead.

criteria.name: empty_collection_export_still_valid
criteria.description: After deleting all events, status rollups show 0 / 0 / 0; Export JSON compiles a valid catalog with an empty events array (leads may still be non-empty); Export ICS is still a valid empty calendar (BEGIN:VCALENDAR and END:VCALENDAR with no VEVENT blocks).

criteria.name: empty_undo_history_inert
criteria.description: With an empty undo history the Undo control is disabled or inert, and activating it produces no state change and no console error.

criteria.name: malformed_import_leaves_collection
criteria.description: Importing a malformed declared-catalog payload leaves the current events collection and leads log unchanged, shows an inline error naming the catalog, and does not blank the manager UI.

criteria.name: bulk_delete_zero_selection_inert
criteria.description: Bulk Delete selected with zero rows selected is inert: no confirmation dialog, and the visible event count does not change.

criteria.name: empty_leads_export_valid_schema
criteria.description: With zero leads, Download leads JSON still produces ridge-session-leads.json with version 1, the current theme, counts.total 0, and an empty leads array; Copy leads JSON copies that same empty-log JSON and still shows Copied confirmation; Undo last lead leaves the empty state unchanged.

criteria.name: invalid_contact_then_valid_appends_one
criteria.description: A failed invalid contact submit followed immediately by a valid contact submit appends exactly one lead — the invalid attempt never appears in Session leads or either export.

criteria.name: module_and_control_radius_tokens
criteria.description: Computed styles expose large module radii and smaller control radii consistent with --ridge-radius-module and --ridge-radius-control rather than arbitrary one-off corner values on every surface.

criteria.name: display_typography_matches_spec
criteria.description: All-caps chapter titles use an expressive geometric display face distinct from body copy; body text remains smaller and secondary across Why Ridge, Get started, and Global Events.

criteria.name: layout_matches_reference_screenshots
criteria.description: At 1440px desktop width, composition matches /reference-screenshots/ overview and segment panels within a small tolerance for sticky chrome, brand-first bento hero, Why Ridge pile, Global Events, and footer density/proportions.

criteria.name: specified_motion_states_present
criteria.description: Spec-required motion states are present via the real UI path: page-load entrance, Why Ridge sticky pile at ≥768px, Get started rise below 1024px, RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS character-decode, and theme toggle rotate/fade.

criteria.name: responsive_patterns_match_spec
criteria.description: Responsive behavior matches the spec: bento stacks on mobile, Why Ridge sticky pile only at ≥768px, hero pull-in at ≥1024px, Get started 1-column mobile / three-up from sm, mega-menu as full-screen drawer on mobile.

criteria.name: notched_controls_and_modules
criteria.description: Light content modules on black fields use dual corner language (large radii plus architectural diagonal notches) and notched CTAs rather than unstyled browser-default buttons.

criteria.name: display_vs_body_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography hierarchy clearly distinguishes oversized all-caps chapter titles from supporting body copy across the landing chapters.

criteria.name: component_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Component states match the spec where specified: theme toggle sun/moon swap, notched CTA hover brighten + arrow nudge, event-row hover wash, and focus-visible rings on interactive controls.

criteria.name: palette_void_ink_accent
criteria.description: Color treatments follow the Ridge system: black-void modules, ink/surface tokens, and a saturated ember/alpine-red accent (not purple) on the geometric mark and interactive accents.

criteria.name: microinteractions_match_spec_timing
criteria.description: Hover/press microinteractions follow the spec language (CTA brighten + arrow nudge, tile lift preserving notch silhouette, theme toggle animate) rather than instant snaps with no transition.

criteria.name: hardened_tools_match_ridge_system
criteria.description: Events Manager rollup/bulk bar, Export catalog panel, Session leads panel, and command palette match the Ridge void/notch and ember-accent system from the reference screenshots' visual language rather than introducing a second unrelated UI kit

criteria.name: session_leads_additive_without_breaking_order
criteria.description: Session leads sits near the footer after conversion forms without removing or reordering the reference landing chapters shown in the screenshots

criteria.name: signature_microinteractions_polished
criteria.description: Notched CTAs brighten with arrow nudge, tiles ease lift while preserving notch silhouette, and event rows take a hover wash — microinteractions feel polished rather than unfinished stubs (verified via real hover on the UI path).

criteria.name: scroll_storytelling_execution
criteria.description: On a fresh load, scroll-triggered storytelling (Why Ridge sticky pile at ≥768px, Get started rise below 1024px, RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS character-decode) executes as a choreographed sequence rather than missing or janky stubs.

criteria.name: hero_load_entrance_execution
criteria.description: The required page-load entrance — sticky chrome sliding down, bento cells via clip-path expansion, hero plane last — executes as a finished one-shot sequence on a fresh load rather than painting fully settled with no entrance.

criteria.name: ridge_brand_narrative_arc
criteria.description: The long-form page reads as one Ridge institutional-tech narrative arc from brand-first hero through Why Ridge, Get started, Network in action, News, Solutions, Community, and Global Events rather than disconnected template blocks.

criteria.name: designed_experience_narrative_arc
criteria.description: The page reads as a designed experience with a narrative arc — black-void modules, dual corner notches, and signature motion build on each other visually, and the Global Events decode is memorable rather than a template with effects sprinkled on.

criteria.name: showcase_rechoreographed_at_narrow_widths
criteria.description: Impeccable execution across breakpoints: at 375px the showcase composition is re-choreographed (stacked bento, full-screen drawer, static Why Ridge list) rather than a broken shrink of the desktop layout.

criteria.name: catalog_operator_tooling_polish
criteria.description: Export JSON/ICS plus ridge-session-leads.json with live session fidelity, undo across surfaces, Session leads operator panel, and command-palette navigation read as polished tooling beyond a static marketing page — verified through the real UI paths

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable enhancement beyond the spec that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.

criteria.name: headings_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings, nav labels, and CTAs, they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout (including all-caps chapter titles such as RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS).

criteria.name: cta_and_manager_labels_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific verbs for Ridge actions (for example View all, Create event, or contact send labels) rather than generic Submit/OK alone when a specific label is possible.

criteria.name: errors_name_field_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders error messages on event create or contact forms, they name the problem field and the fix rather than only saying Invalid.

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders Events Manager empty states (no events remaining, or filters match none), the copy explains what belongs there and how to add an event or clear filters.

criteria.name: marketing_copy_spelling_grammar
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy (hero mission, Why Ridge pillars, Get started, Global Events, Solutions, Community), rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.

criteria.name: ridge_terminology_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders the product name, terminology stays consistent as Ridge (lowercase ridge wordmark where shown) rather than mixing leftover real-network proper nouns.

criteria.name: event_dates_formatted_consistently
criteria.description: Where the app renders event dates in Events Manager rows and Global Events listings, date formatting is consistent across surfaces.

criteria.name: success_messages_state_outcome
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages after a valid contact submit, they state what happened rather than a vague affirmation alone.

criteria.name: institutional_tech_voice
criteria.description: Where the app renders marketing copy, it reads in a coherent institutional-tech voice for an enterprise blockchain product rather than casual template filler.

criteria.name: partner_marks_are_invented_names
criteria.description: Where the app renders trust-strip and partner marks, invented placeholder names appear — never real company logos, trademarks, or real-network proper nouns.

criteria.name: export_import_labels_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders export and import controls, labels use specific verbs (Export JSON, Export ICS, Copy, Download, Load catalog, Download leads JSON, Copy leads JSON, Undo last lead) rather than generic Submit/OK alone

criteria.name: copy_confirmation_states_outcome
criteria.description: Where the app renders Copy confirmation on Export or Session leads, the message states that the catalog or leads JSON was copied rather than a vague OK alone

criteria.name: empty_session_leads_copy_explains
criteria.description: Where the app renders an empty Session leads state, the copy explains that contact submissions will appear there

criteria.name: land_scroll_interact_submit_confirm
criteria.description: Starting on a fresh load: scroll through the landing chapters, open the mega-menu and light-dismiss it, then submit a valid contact form and see an in-page success confirmation without navigating away.

criteria.name: create_event_increments_manager_and_listings
criteria.description: Opening Events Manager and submitting a valid new upcoming event closes the form, increases the manager row count by exactly one, and the same event appears in Global Events landing listings whose status filter matches — all without a reload.

criteria.name: edit_event_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Editing an event's title or status in Events Manager updates that same record everywhere it appears — the manager row, the landing listings, and any featured slot — without a reload.

criteria.name: delete_event_removes_everywhere
criteria.description: Deleting an event removes its row from Events Manager, decreases the visible count by exactly one, and removes it from landing listings and featured slots in the same session.

criteria.name: filter_flow_status_then_category
criteria.description: Applying a status filter in Events Manager narrows the rows; switching to a category filter recomputes the rows from the same shared collection; clearing all filters restores the full seeded-plus-created list exactly.

criteria.name: theme_toggle_recolors_without_reload
criteria.description: Toggling to dark mode recolors the sticky chrome, hero, chapters, and Events Manager together without reloading the document; toggling back restores the light styling; the events collection and any active filters remain unchanged by the toggle.

criteria.name: marketing_and_manager_mode_switch
criteria.description: Switching between Marketing Scroll mode and Events Manager mode (via Global Events View all or a header control) changes the visible context without a full page reload, and returning to Marketing Scroll keeps the shared events collection intact.

criteria.name: mega_menu_open_close_preserves_scroll
criteria.description: Opening the hamburger mega-menu (Build, Solutions, Community, About panels) and closing it via light-dismiss or Escape restores the prior page scroll position and leaves landing chapters operable.

criteria.name: invalid_create_blocks_and_names_field
criteria.description: Submitting an event create with an empty title or missing date does not add an event — the visible count stays unchanged and an inline validation message names the offending field.

criteria.name: reload_returns_seeded_baseline
criteria.description: After creating an event, applying a filter, opening the mega-menu, and toggling theme, a page reload returns the app to its seeded baseline: at least 6 seeded events, default theme, closed mega-menu, and default filters.

criteria.name: export_flow_reflects_session
criteria.description: After creating or editing events and capturing at least one contact lead, opening Export catalog shows a JSON preview whose events and leads arrays reflect those session mutations and an ICS preview with matching VEVENT SUMMARY lines; Copy JSON shows a copied confirmation.

criteria.name: import_round_trip_restores_catalog
criteria.description: Exporting the catalog to JSON, deleting an event so the manager count drops, then importing that JSON restores the prior event set — the same titles and count reappear in Events Manager and matching landing listings.

criteria.name: undo_redo_round_trip_on_delete
criteria.description: Deleting an event and pressing Undo restores it across manager, rollups, and landing listings; pressing Redo removes it again from all three.

criteria.name: bulk_delete_and_sort_flow
criteria.description: Selecting at least two events and choosing Delete selected drops the manager count by the selected count; afterward, sorting the remaining rows by title reorders them from the shared collection without a reload.

criteria.name: command_palette_to_export_catalog
criteria.description: Opening the command palette with Ctrl+K / Cmd+K, choosing Events Manager, then choosing Export catalog reaches those surfaces in-page; Escape closes the palette.

criteria.name: contact_lead_then_download_leads_json
criteria.description: Submitting a valid contact payload shows an in-page success state, appends one Session leads row (total +1), and Download leads JSON produces ridge-session-leads.json whose leads array includes that payload with counts.total matching the panel.

criteria.name: second_lead_export_contains_both
criteria.description: After two successful contact submits with different emails, a subsequent Download leads JSON leads array contains both distinct payloads and counts.total equals 2; Undo last lead then omits the newest from the next export.

criteria.name: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Every interactive control — theme toggle, hamburger, mega-menu links, Events Manager filters, form fields, and CTAs — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), each with a visible focus indicator.

criteria.name: mega_menu_traps_focus_and_returns
criteria.description: Opening the mega-menu drawer traps keyboard focus while open, closes on Escape, and returns focus to the hamburger control on close.

criteria.name: placeholder_media_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Partner marks, trust-strip logos, and other imagery carry descriptive alt text or accessible names; purely decorative marks are hidden from the accessibility tree.

criteria.name: form_validation_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: Event-create and contact-form validation messages are announced via an aria-live polite region as well as shown visually.

criteria.name: form_fields_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: Event Manager create/edit fields and contact/email form fields use explicit label associations (or equivalent accessible names) for every required field.

criteria.name: headings_logical_order
criteria.description: Section headings through the Ridge landing chapters follow a logical order with no skipped levels that break the outline (for example H1 jumping straight to H3).

criteria.name: landmarks_nav_main_footer
criteria.description: The document exposes HTML-first landmarks: a navigation region for sticky chrome / mega-menu, a main content landmark wrapping the landing chapters, and a contentinfo/footer landmark.

criteria.name: contrast_light_and_dark_themes
criteria.description: Body text meets at least 4.5:1 contrast against its surface and UI controls meet at least 3:1 in both light and dark themes, including text on black-void modules and over the hero visual plane.

criteria.name: semantic_interactive_chrome
criteria.description: Theme toggle, hamburger, mega-menu CTAs, manager filters, and form controls use real button/link/input semantics rather than unlabeled non-interactive shells.

criteria.name: reduced_motion_skips_signature_motion
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion emulated on a fresh load, the page-load entrance, Why Ridge sticky pile motion, Get started rise, RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS character-decode, and desktop smooth-scroll are suppressed while the page remains fully scrollable and operable with settled content.

criteria.name: global_events_heading_accessible_name
criteria.description: After the RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS character-decode runs via real enter-view scroll on a fresh load, the heading container exposes the final phrase RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS as its accessible name while individual animated character spans are aria-hidden from the accessibility tree.

criteria.name: command_palette_focus_trap
criteria.description: The open command palette traps keyboard focus while open, closes on Escape, and returns focus to the control that opened it

criteria.name: export_import_status_live_region
criteria.description: Export Copy confirmation, Session leads Copied confirmation, and import error/success messages are announced via an aria-live polite region as well as shown visually

criteria.name: manager_selection_and_undo_keyboard
criteria.description: Events Manager selection checkboxes, bulk actions, sort controls, and undo/redo controls are reachable and operable with the keyboard alone with visible focus indicators

criteria.name: session_leads_controls_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Session leads Undo last lead, Download leads JSON, and Copy leads JSON controls are reachable and operable with the keyboard alone and show a visible focus indicator

criteria.name: cold_start_interactive_under_2s
criteria.description: The page is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load.

criteria.name: console_and_hydration_clean
criteria.description: Loading and exercising the page (scroll-through, theme toggle, mega-menu, Events Manager create/filter, contact submit, session-lead export/copy, Export catalog) produces no console errors, no hydration mismatch errors or warnings, and no unhandled rejections; interactive regions become usable without a visible content flash or layout swap.

criteria.name: chrome_interactions_stay_responsive
criteria.description: Theme toggle, hamburger, and Events Manager mode switch respond without multi-second lag after first paint.

criteria.name: contact_success_does_not_hang_ui
criteria.description: A valid contact submit shows its success state with an animated transition rather than hanging the UI with no feedback.

criteria.name: long_page_scroll_without_lag
criteria.description: Scrolling the full long-form Ridge homepage through every chapter remains usable without perceived multi-second freezes between sections.

criteria.name: interactive_during_manager_and_theme
criteria.description: The UI remains interactive during theme toggles and Events Manager create/delete row updates (controls stay clickable).

criteria.name: animated_chapters_hold_frame_rate
criteria.description: Continuous scrolling from top to bottom holds a smooth frame rate through every animated chapter (Why Ridge pile, Get started rise, Global Events decode).

criteria.name: rapid_theme_toggle_never_freezes
criteria.description: Rapidly toggling the theme several times never freezes the page.

criteria.name: extended_scroll_session_stable
criteria.description: After an extended scroll-through with theme toggles, mega-menu open/close, and Events Manager filtering, the page remains responsive without runaway resource use that freezes interaction.

criteria.name: hero_space_reserved_from_first_paint
criteria.description: After first paint, no visible layout jumps occur as fonts or media finish loading; the hero visual plane holds its reserved space from the start.

criteria.name: scroll_linked_animation_frame_rate
criteria.description: Scroll-linked chapter motion (Why Ridge sticky pile at ≥768px, Global Events enter-view decode, Get started rise below 1024px) holds a smooth frame rate through the full page length on a 1440-wide viewport with no visible hitching during continuous scroll.

criteria.name: desktop_smooth_scroll_feels_continuous
criteria.description: At desktop widths, eased inertial smooth scrolling feels continuous and settles naturally without lag between input and motion, while position:sticky Why Ridge behavior stays intact.

criteria.name: layout_stable_after_load
criteria.description: Layout is stable after load: no visible reflow jumps as fonts or media finish loading — the hero plane and chapter media regions reserve their space from first paint.

criteria.name: rapid_undo_export_responsive
criteria.description: Rapid undo/redo, bulk selection changes, filter/sort toggles, opening Export catalog, and Session leads Download/Copy keep the UI responsive with no hang or dropped interaction

criteria.name: void_notch_composition_intact
criteria.description: The signature layout system is intact: light content modules float on large black void fields with dual corner language (large radii plus architectural diagonal notches) and the brand-first hero composition remains

criteria.name: empty_manager_state_designed
criteria.description: Empty Events Manager state is visually present when no events remain

criteria.name: four_numbered_pillars
criteria.description: The Why Ridge chapter shows exactly four numbered pillars labelled 01, 02, 03, 04

criteria.name: ridge_radius_css_tokens
criteria.description: Reading computed styles on the document root exposes CSS custom properties named --ridge-radius-module (a large radius) and --ridge-radius-control (a small radius), plus void/ink/accent/surface color variables

criteria.name: expressive_display_typeface
criteria.description: All-caps chapter titles render in an expressive geometric display face served from the app's own origin — computed font-family is not Inter, Roboto, Arial, or a system-only stack

criteria.name: single_consistent_icon_set
criteria.description: One consistent icon style appears across the chrome, mega-menu, manager rows, and footer — no visibly mixed icon families

criteria.name: ridge_wordmark_and_title
criteria.description: The header shows a lowercase 'ridge' wordmark beside a sharp geometric mark in a saturated ember/alpine-red accent (not purple), and the document title names Ridge (e.g. 'Custom Blockchains for Enterprise | Ridge')

criteria.name: manager_dense_table_badges
criteria.description: Events Manager mode renders as a dense list/table with visible status badges and filter controls styled in the same visual system as the landing chapters

criteria.name: placeholder_partner_marks_distinct
criteria.description: The trust strip and any client wall show multiple visually distinct, original invented placeholder marks rather than repeated identical placeholders

criteria.name: mobile_375_layout_clean
criteria.description: At 375px width, the bento hero cells stack vertically, no content clips or overflows the viewport, no horizontal scrolling appears, and every chapter, form, and the Events Manager remain reachable

criteria.name: breakpoint_choreography
criteria.description: Breakpoint behavior matches the spec: the mega-menu becomes a full-screen drawer at mobile widths, Get started renders as one column at mobile and three-up from the sm breakpoint, and the hero pull-in composition applies at 1024px and up

criteria.name: consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Headings, nav labels, and CTAs use one consistent capitalization convention throughout the page and the Events Manager

criteria.name: institutional_copy_quality
criteria.description: Marketing copy reads as original, coherent institutional-tech voice for an enterprise blockchain product; error messages name the problem field and the fix, and empty states explain what belongs there and how to add it

criteria.name: why_ridge_static_list_below_768
criteria.description: At a viewport narrower than 768px, the Why Ridge pillars render as a static vertical list — all four pillars fully readable in document order with no sticky stacking or cards covering one another

criteria.name: export_catalog_void_notch_panel
criteria.description: Export catalog renders as a dedicated panel with JSON and ICS preview regions plus Copy/Download/Load controls using the same void/notch visual language as the landing — not a plain unstyled browser dialog

criteria.name: command_palette_ridge_overlay
criteria.description: The command palette is a centered overlay with a search field and listed destinations/actions; the active row is highlighted and the chrome matches Ridge rather than a default OS menu

criteria.name: manager_rollup_and_bulk_bar
criteria.description: Events Manager shows a live status rollup strip and, when rows are selected, a bulk action bar with selected count — both styled in the same visual system as the dense list/table

criteria.name: session_leads_panel_matches_token_system
criteria.description: Session leads panel uses the same void/notch surfaces and ember accent on Download leads JSON / Copy leads JSON / Undo last lead controls as the surrounding conversion forms and footer rather than a visually alien widget

criteria.name: hydration_clean_console
criteria.description: First load and a full exercise of the app produce a console free of errors, hydration mismatch errors or warnings, and unhandled promise rejections, and the interactive islands become usable without throwing

criteria.name: interactive_within_two_seconds
criteria.description: The page is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load

criteria.name: stable_layout_after_load
criteria.description: After first paint, no visible layout jumps occur as fonts or media finish loading, and the hero visual plane holds its reserved space from the start

criteria.name: smooth_full_page_scroll
criteria.description: Continuous scrolling from top to bottom holds a smooth frame rate through every animated chapter with no visible hitching

criteria.name: keyboard_operable_focus_visible
criteria.description: Every interactive control — theme toggle, hamburger, mega-menu links, manager filters, form fields, CTAs — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone and shows a visible focus indicator when focused

criteria.name: mega_menu_focus_trap_escape
criteria.description: While the mega-menu drawer is open, Tab keeps focus inside the drawer; pressing Escape closes it and focus returns to the hamburger control

criteria.name: decoded_headline_accessible_name
criteria.description: The RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS heading exposes the full final phrase as its accessible name while the individually animated characters are hidden from the accessibility tree

criteria.name: validation_announced_aria_live
criteria.description: Form validation messages are exposed through an aria-live polite region in addition to being shown visually

criteria.name: theme_toggle_accessible_name
criteria.description: The theme toggle exposes an accessible name that reflects its current state and updates when toggled

criteria.name: deep_link_parity
criteria.description: Loading the app's URL (and any route or deep link the UI exposes, such as an Events Manager route or section anchor) directly in a fresh tab renders the full page with the same chrome and seeded baseline as reaching it through in-app navigation — no 404s or half-rendered shells

criteria.name: in_memory_export_undo_state
criteria.description: After create/edit/delete, contact lead append, export, undo, and theme toggle, the app keeps working from in-memory client state: Export catalog and ridge-session-leads.json reflect the session and Undo restores mutations without reading browser storage APIs for events/leads/theme

criteria.name: filters_sorts_shared_collection
criteria.description: Status/category filters and date/title sorts recompute the visible Events Manager rows from one shared collection — changing sort after a filter still shows only the filtered membership

criteria.name: lead_log_and_exports_share_one_store
criteria.description: Shared-state coherence: after a successful contact submit, the Session leads list/total, Download leads JSON, and Export JSON leads array agree on counts and payload fields without reload

criteria.name: layout_adapts_1440_to_375
criteria.description: Layout adapts from 1440px desktop to 375px mobile: bento hero cells stack vertically at mobile widths, and every chapter, form, and Events Manager remains reachable.

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets_adequate
criteria.description: At 375px, primary interactive controls (hamburger, theme toggle, form submit, manager filters) present tap targets at least about 44px in height or width.

criteria.name: typography_resizes_across_breakpoints
criteria.description: Display chapter titles and body copy resize between 1440px and 375px without becoming illegible at either width.

criteria.name: no_clip_or_overflow_at_key_widths
criteria.description: At 1440px and 375px, content never clips or overflows the viewport.

criteria.name: mega_menu_becomes_fullscreen_drawer
criteria.description: At mobile widths the mega-menu becomes a full-screen drawer opened from the oversized hamburger; sticky chrome still exposes the Ridge wordmark and theme toggle.

criteria.name: why_ridge_pile_desktop_only
criteria.description: At ≥768px the Why Ridge chapter uses the sticky card pile with progressively larger top offsets; below 768px the four pillars render as a static vertical list.

criteria.name: get_started_column_reflow
criteria.description: Get started renders as 1 column at mobile widths and three-up from the sm breakpoint; the hero pull-in composition applies at 1024px and up.

criteria.name: no_horizontal_scroll_at_375
criteria.description: At 375px width no horizontal scrolling appears.

criteria.name: hero_and_grids_resize
criteria.description: The hero visual plane, Solutions grid, and News carousel size responsively to fit the layout at 1440px and 375px without fixed-size overflow.

criteria.name: sticky_chrome_accessible_all_widths
criteria.description: Sticky top chrome (Ridge wordmark, theme toggle, hamburger) remains reachable at 1440px and 375px without permanently obscuring chapter content.

criteria.name: showcase_mobile_rechoreography
criteria.description: At 375px the brand-first bento hero, Why Ridge pillars, and Global Events chapter are re-choreographed into a stacked mobile composition that preserves hierarchy rather than a broken shrink of the desktop grid.

criteria.name: palette_export_usable_at_375
criteria.description: At 375px width the command palette, Export catalog, and Session leads panel stay fully on-screen and operable rather than clipping off the viewport

criteria.name: bulk_bar_no_horizontal_scroll
criteria.description: At 375px width the bulk action bar wraps or stacks without introducing horizontal scrolling

criteria.name: hover_microinteraction_detail
criteria.description: Hovering with the real pointer: notched CTAs brighten and their arrow nudges, tiles ease into a lift/brightness change while keeping their notch silhouette, event rows take a hover wash, and interactive controls show focus-visible rings when focused via keyboard

criteria.name: mode_switch_keeps_hover
criteria.description: Marketing/Events mode switches update without full reload and keep hover feedback

criteria.name: load_entrance_staggered_sequence
criteria.description: On a fresh first load, the sticky chrome drops in from off-screen top, the two bento cells enter via a clip-path-style expansion with a short vertical settle, and the hero plane enters last — a staggered ease-in-out sequence settling over roughly 1.5-2s rather than painting fully settled

criteria.name: why_ridge_sticky_pile
criteria.description: At desktop width (>=768px), scrolling Why Ridge pins the four pillars into a progressive sticky stack where later cards peek roughly 24-32px below and cover earlier ones

criteria.name: global_events_decode_line_mask
criteria.description: On a fresh load, scrolling into the Ridge Global Events chapter makes the RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS headline flash through decoy glyphs per character before settling, while the supporting blurb reveals line-by-line from behind overflow-hidden masks; the sequence runs once and does not replay on further scrolling

criteria.name: get_started_rise_below_lg
criteria.description: Below 1024px width, the three Get started cards rise from a downward offset into place with an ease-out curve and a visible per-card stagger on enter; at 1024px and up the rise translate is suppressed

criteria.name: theme_toggle_icon_animates
criteria.description: Clicking the theme pill swaps the sun/moon icon with a short rotate/fade transition and the surfaces recolor smoothly rather than snapping between palettes

criteria.name: mega_menu_animates_open_close
criteria.description: Clicking the hamburger animates the mega-menu drawer/full-screen panel in (slide or fade), and a light-dismiss click outside animates it closed rather than snapping

criteria.name: event_row_add_remove_animates
criteria.description: Creating an event through the manager UI animates the new row into the list, deleting an event animates its row out, and the remaining rows ease to their new positions — verified through the real form and delete controls, not an automation state shortcut

criteria.name: form_success_transitions_in
criteria.description: After a valid contact-form submit, the success state transitions in (fade/slide/scale) rather than appearing instantly with no animation

criteria.name: smooth_inertial_scroll
criteria.description: At desktop width, wheel scrolling moves the page with eased, inertial smoothing while position:sticky sections (chrome, Why Ridge pile) continue to pin correctly

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion emulated on a fresh load, the page paints settled with no load entrance, the RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS headline shows its final text immediately, scroll smoothing is disabled, and all content remains complete and reachable

criteria.name: palette_and_export_overlay_transition
criteria.description: Command palette and Export catalog overlays enter and exit with a brief opacity/scale transition rather than snapping; verified via the real UI open/close path

criteria.name: bulk_bar_enters_on_selection
criteria.description: When Events Manager selection becomes non-empty, the bulk action bar slides or fades in rather than appearing with no transition

criteria.name: export_copy_confirmation_animates
criteria.description: Activating Copy on Export or Copy leads JSON shows a brief copied confirmation that appears then resets rather than a permanent static label

criteria.name: kinetic_global_events_character_decode
criteria.description: On a fresh load, real scrolling into Ridge Global Events drives kinetic typography: each character of RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS flashes through decoy glyphs before settling to its final letter (per-character stagger), while the supporting blurb reveals line-by-line from translateY(-100%) inside overflow-hidden wrappers (never a state shortcut; reduced-motion shows final text immediately)

criteria.name: scroll_storytelling_sticky_card_pile
criteria.description: On a fresh load at ≥768px, real scrolling through Why Ridge drives sequential scroll storytelling: the four sticky pillars pin with progressively larger top offsets so later cards cover earlier ones while the chapter title pins above them, advancing in choreographed scroll order rather than a static equal stack

criteria.name: in_memory_multi_facet_reload_resets
criteria.description: Starting on the homepage: toggle theme to dark, open Events Manager and apply a status filter, create one valid upcoming event, and submit one valid contact lead; then reload. All facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline (default theme, closed mega-menu, default filters, at least 6 seeded events without the created row, Session leads total 0) — never a mixed partial survival of some facets.

criteria.name: status_filter_derived_list_changes
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: in Events Manager, apply a status filter and confirm the visible rows change to matching events only; clear the filter and confirm the full list restores exactly.

criteria.name: edit_echoes_manager_and_landing
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: edit an event's title in Events Manager, then view Global Events landing listings without reload; the same updated title appears in both the manager row and matching landing listings.

criteria.name: create_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: note the Events Manager row count, submit one valid new upcoming event, and confirm the count increases by exactly one with no off-by-one lag.

criteria.name: different_event_titles_different_rows
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: create two events with different titles via Events Manager; both rows appear with their respective titles rather than identical hardcoded labels.

criteria.name: interleaved_theme_and_create
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: toggle theme to dark, create a valid event in Events Manager, toggle theme back to light; the new event remains in the manager list and the final theme matches the last toggle — neither flow corrupted the other.

criteria.name: empty_then_repopulate_events
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete events until Events Manager shows its empty state, then create one valid event; the empty state clears and the manager list shows exactly one row while landing listings that match its status update accordingly.

criteria.name: export_then_import_round_trip
criteria.description: Starting from seeded events: create one upcoming event and one contact lead, open Export catalog and capture the JSON preview text, delete that event so the count drops, then import the captured JSON; the created title and prior count return in Events Manager and matching landing listings, and Session leads restore from the packet.

criteria.name: undo_after_delete_restores_export
criteria.description: Delete an event, confirm it is absent from Export JSON, press Undo, and confirm the title reappears in the manager list and in the next Export JSON preview together.

criteria.name: sort_reversal_changes_order
criteria.description: Sort reversal: in Events Manager apply sort by title, note the first visible title, toggle sort direction, and confirm the first visible title changes (order reversed) without altering the underlying collection membership.

criteria.name: rollup_sensitive_to_status_edit
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: note the upcoming and past rollup counts, edit one upcoming event's status to past, and confirm upcoming decreases by one and past increases by one immediately.

criteria.name: lead_export_input_dependent_payloads
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: perform two contact submits with different emails/names; the exported ridge-session-leads.json leads payloads differ accordingly (not byte-identical hardcoded rows).

criteria.name: undo_last_lead_updates_export_and_count
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: after Undo last lead, both the Session leads list/total and a subsequent Copy/Download leads JSON export omit the undone entry and show the decremented counts.total.

criteria.name: theme_mirrored_in_lead_and_catalog_export
criteria.description: Toggle to dark, then Download leads JSON and Export JSON; both report theme dark matching the document; toggle back to light and export again reports theme light.
